About The Role
The Executive, Ecommerce Distribution is a key driver of end-to-end e-commerce order fulfillment excellence within DKSH's Supply Chain Management function. This role ensures that warehouse operations, inventory control, and dispatch activities are executed with precision and efficiency, directly supporting DKSH's commitment to outstanding customer service and e-commerce growth.

What You Will Deliver
Deliver accurate and timely e-commerce order fulfillment by overseeing picking, packing, and staging activities in alignment with customer service levels and delivery cut-off times.Optimize workforce allocation across fulfillment functions to sustain productivity, quality targets, and adequate coverage during peak periods and promotional campaigns.Safeguard inventory accuracy throughout the fulfillment process by resolving stock discrepancies, addressing damaged goods, and escalating shortages before they impact order delivery.Monitor key operational Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) including order fulfillment accuracy, picking and packing productivity, on-time order readiness, and delivery Service Level Agreement (SLA) achievement to drive continuous improvement.Ensure all completed orders are accurately packed, labeled, and ready for timely collection by courier and logistics partners, supported by complete and accurate shipping documentation.Foster a high-performing team culture through daily operational briefings, clear communication of priorities, and consistent supervision of warehouse associates and temporary staff.Uphold warehouse safety standards, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), and housekeeping practices while actively contributing to operational excellence initiatives.

What You Bring
Diploma or Bachelor's Degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related discipline.Minimum 2 to 3 years of experience in warehouse operations, e-commerce fulfillment, logistics, or supply chain operations, with demonstrated exposure to picking, packing, and order fulfillment in a fast-paced environment.Strong planning and resource allocation capabilities to manage operational workloads effectively.Good working knowledge of Warehouse Management Systems (WMS) and inventory control processes.Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet strict operational deadlines.Strong problem-solving and analytical skills to address operational challenges proactively.Effective communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate across teams and engage with stakeholders at all levels.Proficient in Microsoft Excel and warehouse operational reporting.Detail-oriented with a strong focus on accuracy and customer service excellence.

Why Join DKSH
At DKSH, we help companies grow in Asia and enable people to perform at their best. You will be part of an organization that values accountability, collaboration, and long-term partnerships. We offer a dynamic environment where your contributions are visible and where you can build a meaningful career in Supply Chain Management.

jobs.title: Principal Product Manager, Transaction Platform
jobs.description:
Get to Know the Team

The Transaction Platform team powers Grab’s multi-billion-dollar Deliveries business across Southeast Asia. We build the core platforms that orchestrate every order, payment, refund, dispute, and merchant transaction at massive scale. Our mission is to deliver a trusted, resilient, and efficient commerce platform that balances customer experience, merchant trust, and sustainable business growth.

Get to Know the Role 

As a Principal Product Manager, you will define the long-term vision and strategy for Grab’s merchant transaction platform. You’ll lead the evolution of mission-critical systems that power millions of orders daily, working at the intersection of distributed systems, financial integrity, and large-scale operational workflows. This is a highly strategic platform role, requiring deep technical judgment, systems thinking, and the ability to influence engineering, business, and operational leaders across the region.

Key responsibilities

Define Platform Strategy: Own the long-term product vision and roadmap for Grab’s merchant transaction platform, driving architectural investments that enable scale, reliability, and future business growth.

Build High-Scale Platform Capabilities: Design and launch foundational platform services spanning order lifecycle management, merchant operations, dispute resolution, and transactional integrity.

Solve Complex Platform Problems: Lead cross-functional initiatives to simplify complex multi-party workflows, ensuring accurate, reliable, and resilient transaction processing across the ecosystem.

Drive Operational Excellence: Develop intelligent platform capabilities that reduce merchant downtime, improve operational governance, and recover merchant demand through smarter automation, permissions, auditing, and risk controls.

Influence Across the Organization: Partner with engineering, operations, finance, legal, and commercial leaders to align technical investments with business priorities and drive execution across multiple teams.

Regional Product Manager, Medical Devices

Build and scale mandateRegional remit across SEA and Korea

On behalf of a Fortune 500 medical devices company with an emerging footprint in Southeast Asia, we are searching for a Regional Product Manager to lead a genuine greenfield build. Our client operates at significant scale globally, and in this region, it runs like an incubator: a lean setup tasked with growing a surgical portfolio from a low base into a real regional presence, market by market, starting in Singapore before expanding further. This is a rare opportunity for a product leader to run an incubator-style mandate inside an established global business.

This is a build mandate, not a maintenance role.

The successful candidate will own the full arc, from market assessment and collaboration with the global sourcing hub, through portfolio and go-to-market strategy, to hands-on downstream execution with distributors and sales teams. Equal comfort building a business case and standing in an operating theatre discussing workflows with a scrub nurse or surgeon is essential.

Core responsibilities:

Assess market opportunity and shape portfolio strategy across SEA, working closely with the global sourcing hubBuild and execute go-to-market plans market-by-market, starting with SingaporeServe as the link between global product teams and in-market commercial executionDevelop genuine fluency in the surgical environment, workflows, clinical needs, and what it takes to displace entrenched incumbentsTrain, enable and support field teams and distributors as the portfolio scales

Desired skills and competencies:

Over 10 years in product management, marketing, or commercial roles in medical devices.Real, hands-on understanding of the Operating Room and peri-operative environment. A surgical consumables background is strongly preferred.A track record of building (not just running) a market, portfolio, or function from limited infrastructureStrong stakeholder and distributor management skills across a multicultural, matrixed environmentWillingness to travel frequently across the region

Thank you for your interest. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. All resumes collected will be used for recruitment purposes only.

jobs.title: Technical Product Owner
jobs.description:
Work Schedule

Standard (Mon-Fri)

Environmental Conditions

Office

Job Description

As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.

DESCRIPTION

How you will make an impact:

As a Technical Product Owner, you will play a critical role in delivering software and automation solutions for Cell and Gene Therapy and laboratory automation workflows. You will own product requirements, drive backlog prioritization, align stakeholders, and support delivery of business-critical initiatives, including automated workflow solutions, API-enabled system integration, and AI-assisted software development practices.

This role sits at the intersection of science, software engineering, automation, and customer engagement. You will partner closely with engineering teams, business stakeholders, quality, project leaders, and customer-facing teams to translate complex workflow needs into clear product requirements, acceptance criteria, and scalable software capabilities. You will also help shape future product direction by supporting API-first architecture, interoperability across automation platforms, and future lab integration.

What you will do:

• Own and manage product requirements, product specifications, and acceptance criteria for software and automation solutions

• Prioritize product backlog items, defects, and feature requests in alignment with business priorities and delivery milestones

• Drive stakeholder alignment across engineering, quality, project management, business, and customer-facing teams

• Support release planning, readiness reviews, acceptance testing, and customer-facing documentation

• Serve as a key product contact for strategic collaborations, gathering feedback and refining requirements

• Manage stakeholder expectations, issue resolution, and requirement clarification to maintain delivery confidence and product quality

• Partner with engineering teams to ensure requirements are testable, traceable, and ready for implementation

• Define and support API-first product strategy for scalable integration across systems and automation platforms

• Bridge Cell and Gene Therapy domain knowledge, instrumentation workflows, and software design principles

• Enable interoperability across laboratory automation platforms and future lab ecosystem initiatives

• Drive AI-enabled product definition, requirement workflows, and AI-SDLC adoption

• Establish governance and guardrails for AI-assisted product and software development practices

• Improve consistency, quality, and scalability of requirement management and product documentation

REQUIREMENTS

How you will get here:

• Bachelor’s degree in Software Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Life Sciences, Computer Science, Automation, or a related technical field

• 6+ years of experience in product development, software product ownership, systems engineering, automation, or related technical roles

• Experience owning product requirements, specifications, acceptance criteria, backlog prioritization, and release readiness

• Experience working with software engineering teams through the product development lifecycle

• Strong ability to translate customer, scientific, and business needs into clear technical requirements

• Experience leading cross-functional alignment across engineering, quality, project management, business, and customer-facing teams

• Strong technical documentation, analytical, and problem-solving skills

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage technical and non-technical stakeholders

• Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, matrixed environment

• Experience with API design, system integration, laboratory automation, or industrial automation preferred

• Familiarity with Cell and Gene Therapy workflows, instrumentation, or regulated product environments is a plus

• Knowledge of quality systems, verification and validation, GMP, GxP, ISO, or 21 CFR Part 11 is advantageous

• Familiarity with AI-enabled development workflows, requirement management tools, or AI-SDLC practices is advantageous

• Team-oriented mindset with strong interpersonal skills and a bias for clear ownership and execution

jobs.title: Staff I4.0 Product Owner
jobs.description:
Our vision is to transform how the world uses information to enrich life for all.

Join an inclusive team passionate about one thing: using their expertise in the relentless pursuit of innovation for customers and partners. The solutions we build help make everything from virtual reality experiences to breakthroughs in neural networks possible. We do it all while committing to integrity, sustainability, and giving back to our communities. Because doing so can fuel the very innovation we are pursuing.

As an Industry 4.0 (I4.0) Product Owner at Micron Technology, Inc., you will lead the definition, development, and delivery of end-to-end smart manufacturing solutions to empower Micron Assembly and Test Operation to improve cost, scale, quality and productivity. You will bridge business, engineering, and data science teams to drive Industry 4.0 transformation, leveraging industry best methods, advanced analytics, automation, and AI/ML to optimize planning and manufacturing operations. This role requires deep expertise in I4.0 technologies and strong hands-on data science skills to deliver impactful, scalable solutions across global operations.

Key Responsibilities

Product Ownership & I4.0 Leadership

Define and drive the I4.0 product roadmap, aligning with business objectives and manufacturing strategies.Lead cross-functional teams (engineering, IT, data science, operations) to deliver integrated smart manufacturing solutions.Gather, analyze, and prioritize requirements from end users and stakeholders, develop business cases, validate impact, and propose high-ROI projects for implementation.Understands business problems and effectively translates user requirements into technical specifications—and vice versa—for data science, technical solution, and IT teams.Collaborate with global teams to deploy and maintain data science solutions in production environments, ensure data quality, integrity, and continuous improvement across all relevant data sources.Document artifacts related to software development, design, test cases, user training and releases.Communicate effectively with peers, senior management, and external partners.Present to small and large groups, demonstrating strong leadership and relationship management skills.Ensure optimization and standardization of tools and processes by leveraging central team business partner to enable sustainable scaling across sites.

Data Science & Analytics

Provide expert guidance on system interfaces, capabilities, and process improvements.Design and develop intuitive and responsive user interface for interactive data visualization to explain insights from data or analytic models.Create wireframes, prototypes and UI mockups based on business requirements and user feedback.Collaborate with backend and frontend developers to define API specifications and deliver robust solutions.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Industrial Engineering, Information Technology, or related field.5+ years of experience in smart manufacturing, process engineering, or similar operations, with proven I4.0 project delivery.Proven experience leading cross-functional and global teams, with strong collaboration skills across peers, senior, and mid-level management.Demonstrated experience in driving digital transformation initiatives within manufacturing or industrial environments.Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills.Strong communication skills (written, verbal, and presentation).3+ years of hands-on experience in data science, analytics, or related roles. Strong knowledge of data engineering (ETL, data warehousing, SQL, database management systems such as Snowflake).Proficiency in web development (Angular, JavaScript/Typescript, HTML) and business intelligence tools (Tableau, Power Platform). Experience with APIs, JSON/XML data formats, and Agile development processes.Strong domain knowledge in capacity modeling, planning and optimization, or line management and manufacturing performance monitoring.Knowledge of semiconductor manufacturing processes and systems (e.g., MES, SAP) is a plus.

Preferred Attributes

Self-motivated, with strong ownership and ability to work independently.High attention to detail, data accuracy, and quality.Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.Highly organized and ability to remain flexible and adapt to changing business needs.Passion for innovation and continuous improvement in manufacturing.

jobs.title: Product Manager - Trading Experience | Singapore, SG
jobs.description:
Product Manager - Trading Experience

Who We Are 

Axi is a leading global provider of margin and deliverable Foreign Exchange, Contracts for Difference (CFDs), and Financial Spread betting. Our business has evolved into a world-class, multifaceted brokerage with offices in six regions. With heavy investment in the latest trading technology, Axi seeks to offer the most comprehensive end-to-end trading experience available, servicing traders of all levels from beginners to institutional-level clients. 
Let's talk about the cool stuff you will do at Axi! 

As a Product Manager - Trading Experience, you will own and enhance key product experiences across Axi's app and web trading platforms, with a focus on how traders discover opportunities, place trades, manage positions, monitor performance, and interact with the broader trading lifecycle. 

This is a high-impact product role for someone who understands, or has a strong passion for, trading products and trader behaviour. You will work closely with engineering, design, data, trading, risk, middle office, eFX, operations, compliance, and commercial teams to build product experiences that are fast, intuitive, reliable, and aligned to what traders need. 

You will shape product capabilities across trading journeys, order placement, position management, watchlists, charts, alerts, trade history, account visibility, execution experience, platform performance, and trader engagement. You will also work closely with internal teams to ensure the front-end trading experience is supported by robust middle office, execution, reconciliation, risk, and operational processes. 

This role is well suited to a Product Manager who has worked on trading platforms, brokerage products, fintech platforms, investment apps, FX/CFD products, or other complex financial products. We are also open to strong Product Managers from adjacent digital product environments who have a genuine personal interest in trading and can demonstrate strong understanding of trader needs, trading behaviour, and platform expectations. 
Your EDGE Assignment / You Will 

• Define, own, and continuously refine the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for Axi's app and web trading experience, ensuring alignment with company priorities, client needs, trading performance, and commercial objectives. 
• Build and improve trader-facing product experiences, including market discovery, watchlists, charts, order tickets, trade execution flows, open positions, order management, trade history, alerts, account metrics, margin visibility, and portfolio performance. 
• Understand what traders need from a digital trading experience, including speed, stability, transparency, confidence, ease of use, execution clarity, pricing accuracy, and access to relevant trading information. 
• Partner closely with engineering, design, data, trading, eFX, middle office, risk, operations, compliance, support, and commercial stakeholders to deliver trading product initiatives end-to-end. 
• Work with middle office and operational teams to understand trade lifecycle processes, including trade capture, validation, reconciliation, exposure monitoring, reporting, and issue resolution. 
• Collaborate with eFX and trading teams to understand execution-related considerations such as pricing, liquidity, latency, slippage, order routing, rejected trades, trade flow, and platform performance. 
• Translate trader needs, business requirements, technical constraints, and operational considerations into clear product requirements, priorities, user stories, workflows, and well-managed backlogs for engineering teams. 
• Use data, client feedback, trading behaviour, support insights, funnel metrics, platform analytics, and qualitative research to identify friction points and prioritise product improvements. 
• Lead the development and rollout of new features, capabilities, and product improvements that improve the trading experience across app and web platforms. 
• Monitor product performance through adoption, engagement, conversion, execution-related metrics, trade activity, client feedback, incident trends, and operational impact. 
• Ensure product capabilities meet relevant regulatory, compliance, risk, and operational requirements, especially where trading, execution, leverage, margin, risk warnings, promotions, and client communications are involved. 
• Communicate clearly and proactively with stakeholders on roadmap progress, product priorities, trade-offs, delivery risks, client impact, and business outcomes. 
• Balance trader experience, commercial impact, regulatory expectations, technical feasibility, operational resilience, and delivery constraints to make sound product decisions. Are you the one? 

• You have 7+ years of total work experience, with most of that experience in product management, ideally across trading platforms, fintech, brokerage, investing, FX, CFDs, wealth, payments, or similarly complex digital products. 
• You have experience building or improving app and/or web product experiences used by customers in high-intent, high-frequency, or financially sensitive environments. 
• You understand, or have a strong personal interest in, trading products and trader behaviour, including what traders expect from a platform before, during, and after placing a trade. 
• You have knowledge of trading platform experiences such as market search, watchlists, charts, order placement, open positions, order history, account balances, margin usage, P&L, alerts, deposits, withdrawals, and client reporting. 
• You have a proven track record of owning and delivering successful software products, features, or customer experiences across web and/or mobile platforms. 
• You are comfortable working across the full product development lifecycle, from discovery and problem definition through to delivery, launch, measurement, and continuous improvement. 
• You have strong product sense, with the ability to define strategy while also driving execution at the detail level. 
• You can translate complex trading, operational, technical, and commercial requirements into simple, scalable, and user-friendly product experiences. 
• You are confident using customer insights, product analytics, trading data, stakeholder input, market research, competitor analysis, and user feedback to inform decisions and improve outcomes. 
• You communicate well with both technical and non-technical stakeholders and can influence cross-functional teams across product, engineering, trading, operations, risk, and compliance. 
• You have strong prioritisation skills and can manage multiple complex initiatives while making clear trade-offs. 
• You have strong ownership, sound judgement, and a bias towards action.

jobs.title: Product Owner, Reporting & MI (Singapore, Malaysia)
jobs.description:
Job Summary

This role could be based in Singapore or Malaysia. 

We are seeking a talented and passionate, individual to join Standard Chartered as the Product Owner of Reporting and MI in the Markets COO Post Trade Transformation team. 
• Our Product Owners have a deep understanding of the target market/industry and build close relationships with key stakeholders to ensure the teams effort is aligned with overall strategy and client / customer needs. 

The Markets COO Post Trade Transformation team drives and executes the overall strategic transformation on various Post Trade capabilities (Settlement, Clearing, Margining, Documentation, Business and Reference static data management, etc…)
The Product Owner works under the guidance of the Chief Product Owner Post Trade, Alexandre Nguyem
If tackling complex challenges excites you, then join our Markets COO Post Trade Transformation team where you will get to collaborate and work on solutions across business and functions.

You will have the opportunity to build and maintain the best-in-class products / services for our clients and customers, harness the power of AI whilst continuously striving to reduce time-to-market and streamline our processes.

Key Responsibilities

The Product Owner for Reporting and Business Intelligence is an empowered decision maker, accountable for maximizing the business value of reporting, management information and analytics capabilities across the trade lifecycle.
They define and deliver self-service reporting capabilities for end users, enabling operations teams to access timely, accurate and actionable insights to manage processing activities, risks and performance.
The role focuses on modernising reporting and data consumption across operations, reducing reliance on manual reporting and fragmented data sources, and enabling scalability across multiple processing capabilities.
The Product Owner represents the voice of operations, management and stakeholders throughout the lifecycle of the product, ensuring reporting solutions are fit-for-purpose, intuitive and aligned to business needs.
They are a core member of the team, fully dedicated to the role, and accountable for a unified backlog aligned to reporting and business intelligence outcomes.

Accountable for:
• Maximising business value by ensuring confirmations processes deliver the right outcomes at the right time, balancing efficiency, client experience, and risk
• Driving the reduction of manual processing through automation and standardisation across products and regions
• Ensuring confirmations are accurate, timely, and compliant with regulatory and control requirements
• Balancing new features, operational improvements, and risk remediation while maintaining a low cost of change
• Delivering measurable outcomes aligned to business objectives, including efficiency gains, risk reduction, and improved client satisfaction
• Optimising value through continuous feedback from operations, clients, and stakeholders, and ongoing refinement of priorities.

• Owning and managing a single confirmations backlog:
• Clearly defining backlog items in a structured format with clear acceptance criteria
• Continuously prioritising based on value, risk, and operational impact
• Using data such as volumes, processing times, errors, and exceptions to inform decisions
• Ensuring backlog visibility and transparency to all stakeholders
• Acting as the primary interface between business, operations, and technology teams to ensure delivery of end-to-end outcomes
• Ensuring solutions are scalable, standardised, and aligned across products, avoiding fragmented or bespoke implementations
• Managing operational risk, process changes, and delivery risks associated with the Payments capability

Business
Define and maintain the reporting and business intelligence product vision and roadmap, aligned to post-trade transformation priorities and operational scalability goals
Drive the development of self-service capabilities, enabling users to:
• Access data independently
• Create and consume reports without manual intervention
• Monitor performance and risks in real time
• Modernise and simplify the management information landscape, reducing fragmentation and duplication across reporting solutions
• Establish a single source of truth for operational data, ensuring consistency, accuracy and transparency across stakeholders
• Enable data-driven decision making across operations, supporting management with clear visibility on volumes, performance, risks and exceptions
• Promote standardisation of reporting metrics and definitions across capabilities to ensure alignment and comparability
• Drive automation of data extraction, aggregation and reporting processes to reduce manual effort and improve timeliness
• Collaborate with other Product Owners to ensure reporting capabilities are integrated across the full trade lifecycle, from execution to post-trade processing
• Leverage data and user feedback to continuously refine reporting capabilities and prioritise high-value use cases

Processes
Ensure alignment of reporting and data processes with enterprise standards across the trade lifecycle
Own and continuously improve end-to-end reporting workflows, including:
• Data sourcing and aggregation
• Data validation and quality controls
• Report generation and distribution
• Dashboard creation and self-service enablement
• Ensure strong data quality, governance and auditability, with clear ownership and traceability of data
Enable efficient workflows by:
• Reducing manual data manipulation
• Simplifying access to data
• Standardising report generation processes
Partner closely with operations teams to:
• Understand reporting needs and pain points
• Validate usability and effectiveness of reporting solutions
• Ensure adoption of self-service capabilities
• Maintain clear visibility on dependencies with upstream data providers and downstream consumers
• Ensure all governance, documentation and assurance standards are met for reporting and analytics solutions

People and Talent 
• Champion and act as a role model of the Group’s values and culture. Lead through example and build the appropriate culture and values. Sets appropriate tone and expectations for their team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners.
• Lead and support a change in mindset, building a culture of client centricity, agility, and accountability through standardised metrics and measurement, ensuring everyone in the Bank are drivers and owners of client outcomes.
• Set effective metrics and standards, transparently communicating them to team members, providing feedback and rewarding employees accordingly. Set the appropriate tone and expectations for the wider team.
• Employ, engage, and retain high quality people, with succession planning for critical roles.
• Ensure team structure/capacity is reviewed to enable delivery of client outcomes. 
• Ensure the provision of on-going training & development, ensure that direct reports are suitably skilled and qualified for their roles and that they have effective supervision in place to mitigate any risk.
• Set and monitor job descriptions and objectives for direct reports and provide feedback and rewards in line with their performance against those responsibilities and objectives.
• Create a motivational and challenging environment for staff which is conducive to creativity and performance with customer centricity in mind.
• Provide feedback on transformation leaders to their people leaders, as part of performance reviews

Risk Management
• Create transparency around backlog item progress, blockers, impediments, dependencies, and product value delivery via fit for purpose business and delivery metrics. 
• Engage process and risk control owners in upfront in design of process changes. Have oversight of, direct tracking and monitoring of projects. 

Governance 
• Adopt and embed the Ways of Working Standards throughout the lifecycle of the product / service.
• Awareness and understanding of the regulatory framework in which the Group operates, and the regulatory requirements and expectations relevant to the role.

Regulatory & Business Conduct 
• Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. 
• Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct.
• Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters.

Key stakeholders

• T&O Markets & Post Trade Technology Leadership team 
• Markets COO Leadership team 
• Markets Trading and Sales 
• Markets Operations Leadership team 
• Operations BAU teams across Middle-Office, Post Trade, Clearing, Margining, etc… 
• Credit and Market Risk teams
• Functional Partners : CFCC, HR, Finance, Legal, GIA
• Transformation Leads across the Bank
• Group Process owners

Skills and Experience

• Academic or Professional Education/Qualifications: As per Bank’s requirements and HR policy
• Licences and Certifications/accreditations: 
• Product Owner certification from Scrum Alliance, Scrum.org or other associations are must
• Practitioner certification of Scaled Agile framework like LeSS, SAFe, Scrum@Scale, Spotify, Nexus and Human Centred Design or Design Thinking are plus.
• Professional Memberships: Association or Discipline Association Membership well regarded.

Our Ideal Candidate

• 5 plus years of experience in Financial Markets operations, post-trade processing or capability delivery, with exposure to reporting, data or analytics
• Solid understanding of the end-to-end trade lifecycle, with the ability to connect operational processes to reporting and management information needs
• Proven experience in reporting, data analysis or business intelligence, with strong understanding of how operational data is structured, transformed and consumed
• Hands-on experience as a Product Owner or equivalent role, with strong capability in:
• Managing and prioritising a backlog
• Driving delivery outcomes across cross-functional teams
• Translating business needs into data and reporting solutions
• Proven ability to:
• Define clear requirements for reporting and analytics use cases
• Translate complex data needs into actionable insights and user-friendly outputs
• Apply prioritisation and decision-making techniques effectively
• Strong understanding of operational metrics, including volumes, performance, exceptions and risk indicators
• Demonstrated experience working with globally distributed teams, including operations, technology and business stakeholders
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to interpret data and drive decision making.

• Articulate reporting needs and product vision clearly
• Influence stakeholders on data-driven priorities
• Ensure transparency on data issues and resolution plans
• Good knowledge of Agile ways of working, including iterative delivery, backlog refinement and continuous improvement
• A forward-looking, adaptable mindset, with a strong focus on modernisation, simplification and scalability
• Awareness of how to leverage automation and emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence, to enhance reporting capabilities, improve data access and enable smarter insights
• Familiarity with user-centric design approaches, ensuring reporting tools and dashboards are intuitive and drive adoption

Role Specific Technical Competencies

• Product / Service subject matter expertise 
• Product delivery lifecycle experience using Agile Ways of Working (Scrum, Kanban, Lean, etc…)
• Understanding Customer Needs (research, insights & communication)
• Data driven decision making & product analytics 
• Business plan – design and execution (tactical and strategic)
• Marketing Strategy and Positioning
• Stakeholder management and communication
• Regulatory Environment – Financial Services 

About Standard Chartered

We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before.

If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us.

Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion.

Together we:

Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do

Never settle, continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well

Are better together, we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long...

jobs.title: Digital & eCommerce D2C Specialist APAC
jobs.description: Digital and eCommerce D2C Specialist Salary: S$5,000 - S$5,500 Base Salary per month Location: Singapore Keywords: D2C strategies, eCommerce growth, digital marketing, data analysis, APAC region, customer journey, omnichannel opportunities, UX-UI optimisation, cross-functional collaboration, performance metricsThis is an exceptional opportunity for you to join a globally recognised lifestyle technology leader as a Digital and eCommerce Direct to Consumer Specialist, based in Singapore. As part of a multi-disciplinary team, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the digital landscape across Asia Pacific, connecting consumers with premium audio experiences through innovative technology. This position offers the chance to work with high-end brands renowned for their quality and innovation, while driving forward-thinking D2C strategies that directly impact business growth. You will enjoy a flexible work environment, access to exclusive employee discounts on world-class products, and extensive training opportunities designed to support your professional development. The organisation is committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace where your contributions are valued and your career aspirations are supported. If you are passionate about digital transformation, eager to collaborate with talented colleagues across the region, and ready to make a tangible difference in the world of eCommerce, this role provides the perfect platform for your ambitions. Join a global team at the forefront of digital innovation in consumer electronics, working collaboratively to deliver superior online experiences and drive direct-to-consumer growth across Asia Pacific. Benefit from a flexible working environment, generous employee discounts on premium products, comprehensive training programmes through an internal university, and a culture that celebrates achievement and personal development. Play a key role in developing and executing data-driven D2C strategies, leveraging cutting-edge tools and collaborating with cross-functional teams to optimise customer journeys and maximise commercial performance. What you'll do: As a Digital and eCommerce Direct to Consumer Specialist based in Singapore, you will be instrumental in shaping the future of digital commerce across Asia Pacific. Your day-to-day responsibilities will revolve around crafting impactful D2C strategies that not only drive online sales but also foster deeper connections between consumers and premium audio brands. You will be entrusted with optimising every aspect of the customer journey—leveraging advanced CRM systems, marketing automation tools, loyalty programmes, and personalisation tactics—to create memorable experiences that encourage repeat engagement. By identifying B2B opportunities within various channels and collaborating closely with both internal stakeholders and external partners such as distributors or local market leads, you will help unlock new avenues for revenue growth. Your expertise in data analysis will enable you to monitor sales performance, traffic patterns, competitor activities, and industry developments so you can proactively recommend improvements or capitalise on emerging trends. Through close collaboration with global teams on site optimisation projects as well as hands-on involvement in UX-UI enhancements using state-of-the-art tools, you will ensure that all digital touchpoints are intuitive and conversion-focused. Additionally, your ability to develop integrated media plans—spanning both paid advertising platforms like social media giants as well as organic channels—will be crucial in promoting new product launches while maintaining strong ROAS. Regularly generating detailed reports on campaign effectiveness will allow you to communicate successes clearly with stakeholders at all levels. Ultimately, your efforts will contribute significantly towards building an agile digital ecosystem that supports sustainable business growth throughout the region. Develop and implement comprehensive end-to-end direct-to-consumer (D2C) strategies aimed at increasing online sales, acquiring new customers, and strengthening brand presence throughout the Asia Pacific region. Optimise every stage of the consumer journey by utilising CRM systems, marketing automation tools, loyalty programmes, and personalisation initiatives to enhance customer lifetime value and drive incremental revenue through targeted campaigns. Identify B2B opportunities within various channels to generate additional revenue streams for D2C operations while balancing relationships between offline retail partners and eCommerce platforms. Maximise commercial performance of company websites by adopting a data-driven approach to identify emerging trends, spot new opportunities, and ensure optimal product assortment and pricing strategies. Lead the planning and execution of APAC-wide promotional activities such as Gift with Purchase (GWP) campaigns and exclusive merchandise rollouts in collaboration with local market leads and distributors. Collaborate closely with global teams to define requirements for on-site optimisation on Salesforce Commerce Cloud-enabled D2C sites as well as other eCommerce platforms supporting regional distributors. Utilise UX-UI tools to analyse user behaviour on digital platforms, identifying pain points in navigation or interface design to ensure seamless user experiences that result in high conversion rates. Conduct A/B testing on website elements, marketing messages, and user experiences; leverage insights from these tests alongside broader data analysis to inform decision-making processes. Build robust e-commerce calendars, assortments, and strategic plans together with business development managers and country-specific teams or distributors for both direct and indirect eCommerce channels. Develop integrated digital media plans using paid channels (such as Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Google) alongside non-paid channels (including email marketing), manage agency relationships for post-campaign analysis, track return on ad spend (ROAS), promote new product launches, attract new customers, retain existing ones, and provide comprehensive reporting for all D2C environments. What you bring: To excel as a Digital and eCommerce Direct to Consumer Specialist in this organisation’s Singapore office, you should bring proven experience navigating complex digital landscapes while demonstrating deep technical acumen across multiple eCommerce platforms. Your academic background should include formal qualifications relevant to digital marketing or commerce disciplines. Practical experience implementing successful D2C initiatives—especially those tailored for APAC audiences—will set you apart. You should possess strong analytical skills enabling you to interpret large volumes of data quickly so that informed decisions can be made efficiently. Familiarity with UX-UI principles will allow you to advocate effectively for user-centric improvements across all digital touchpoints. Experience gained within consumer electronics or retail industries would provide valuable context when engaging with cross-functional teams or external partners; however candidates from other sectors who demonstrate adaptability are also encouraged to apply. Your up-to-date knowledge regarding current industry trends ensures that your recommendations remain relevant amidst rapid technological change. In addition to technical competencies around analytics & performance marketing tools (both paid & organic), your interpersonal skills must support effective collaboration within culturally-diverse teams spread across multiple geographies. The ability to communicate findings persuasively—whether presenting campaign results internally or negotiating priorities externally—will further enhance your impact within this dynamic environment. Bachelor’s degree in Digital Marketing, eCommerce or a related field is required for this position. A minimum of two years’ experience driving direct-to-consumer growth within digital commerce environments is essential. Demonstrated proficiency with leading eCommerce platforms such as Shopify, Magento, WooCommerce or Salesforce Commerce Cloud is necessary for success in this role. Experience utilising UX-UI methodologies or tools to improve customer journeys is highly desirable. Background in consumer electronics or general retail sectors would be advantageous but not mandatory. Comprehensive knowledge of digital marketing best practices within dotcom environments coupled with an understanding of evolving consumer behaviours is expected. Up-to-date awareness of industry trends along with familiarity regarding emerging D2C technologies is important for ongoing strategy development. Analytical mindset capable of interpreting complex datasets; proven ability to derive actionable insights from data analysis is critical. Track record of successfully developing & executing D2C strategies that deliver measurable business results—particularly within APAC markets—is highly valued. Proficiency using analytics platforms combined with expertise in both performance marketing & non-paid digital strategies is required. What sets this company apart: This organisation stands out by offering an inclusive culture where diversity is celebrated and professional growth is actively encouraged through continuous learning opportunities such as access to its own internal university. Employees benefit from a flexible work environment designed around individual needs rather than rigid schedules—enabling better work-life balance without compromising productivity or ambition. Generous employee discounts provide access to some of the world’s most respected audio products at preferential rates while regular recognition programmes ensure outstanding contributions never go unnoticed. The company’s commitment extends beyond just business results: it fosters genuine connections among colleagues through collaborative projects spanning multiple countries & disciplines—all united by shared values centred around respect & mutual support. Whether you’re looking for structured career progression pathways or simply want space where your ideas can flourish alongside likeminded professionals passionate about technology & innovation—this employer provides everything needed for long-term satisfaction & success. What's next: If you are excited by the prospect of shaping the future of digital commerce across Asia Pacific while enjoying unparalleled support for your professional development—this could be your next great career move! Apply today by clicking on the link provided below; take the first step towards joining an inspiring team dedicated to redefining what’s possible within the world of digital commerce.Do note that we will only be in touch if your application is shortlisted. jobs.title: Principal Product Manager (Singapore Based)
jobs.description:
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world. 

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

Get to know our team: In Product, ideas come alive. The world is moving fast so our culture empowers ownership and minimal bureaucracy. That’s the environment that enables you to do what you think is right – and quickly. Our Product Managers move fast, fail, learn, and pivot. Being a product-centric organization, our team works cross-functionally to build products from conception to launch by bringing together engineering and business worlds. Agoda Product Managers work closely with executive leadership and have high visibility among the C-suite; they have direct impact on Agoda’s growth and strategy. Product puts our travelers first and delivers that special travel experience in the most creative expression.

The opportunity:This role is SINGAPORE-BASED, Our Principal Product Managers are analytical, data-driven product leaders who independently drive large, complex initiatives end-to-end. In this role, you will lead the Trip Planner team, shaping a multi-quarter to multi-year vision for how travelers plan and manage their whole trip with Agoda.

As our Trip Planner Principal Product Manager, you will lead a group of Product Managers and partner closely with senior leaders across Product, Engineering, Design, Data, and business functions to define strategy and align stakeholders around a clear roadmap for the trip canvas and cross-product travel experiences.

You will have dedicated engineering capacity that you are accountable for directing and prioritizing, and are expected to be hands-on with your teams, with strong technical understanding of Agoda’s systems. You will champion a culture that balances traveler experience with KPI optimization and quality, driving cross-team initiatives that meaningfully improve our Trip Planner product.

Beyond your immediate scope, you are an agent of change and a culture carrier: you initiate better ways of working and you help develop other PMs by modeling Agoda’s product principles and leadership behaviors.

In this role, you’ll get to: 
Lead and develop a group of Product Managers, providing clear direction, coaching, and actionable feedback.
Shape product vision and roadmap aligned with Agoda’s business goals.
Analyze the online travel ecosystem, markets, competitive landscape and user requirements.
Conceptualize, launch and optimize products and features, experiment, test their performance and iterate quickly.

jobs.title: Omnichannel Growth Lead
jobs.description:
As the Omnichannel Growth Lead, you will be responsible for optimizing commercial strategies, deciding how products and innovations are delivered through various channels to maximize sales, transaction growth, and profitability across Japan and Korea. This leadership role focuses on commercial optimization rather than technical management.

The Omnichannel Growth Lead is a strategic leadership role responsible for orchestrating the synergy between digital platforms and retail execution across North Asia. This role leads the "Where & How" of our commercial strategy—defining how our brand and products are activated across all customer touchpoints to maximize sales growth and channel profitability.

The job functions of this role include but are not limited to:

I. Driving Omnichannel Growth & Strategy

Omnichannel Orchestration: Lead the regional strategy to integrate digital and physical channels, ensuring a seamless and high-converting customer journey across Kiosk, App, Web, and Delivery.

Commercial Growth: Partner with the Retail & Menu Strategy teams to take product innovations and "orchestrate" their activation across all channels to maximize reach and conversion.

Channel Optimization: Define the strategic role and priority for each sales channel to drive overall business performance and digital sales mix.

Delivery Strategy: Lead the partnership with 3rd party delivery aggregators to maximize growth opportunities and optimize the commercial delivery model.

II. Commercial Excellence & Analytics

Business Performance: Define and track key performance metrics to drive the discipline of consistent channel growth and business targets.

Unit Economics: Leverage data and business analytics to optimize profitability, including average ticket value, gross margin, and channel-specific promotional mechanics.

Data-Led Recommendations: Establish performance dashboards and frameworks to provide sharp, executive-level business recommendations that influence regional strategy.

III. Next-Gen Customer Experience & Loyalty

Digital Transformation: Lead the commercial roadmap for next-gen digital platforms and restaurant technology to ensure they deliver immediate business ROI.

CRM & Loyalty: Drive the regional loyalty and CRM strategy to increase customer frequency, engagement, and long-term lifetime value.

CX/ UX Leadership: Champion a reliable and easy ordering experience across all platforms, identifying opportunities for user experience enhancements and channel optimization.

IV. Franchisee & Regional Partnership

Franchisee Alignment: Act as the primary subject matter expert and advisor to franchisees on omnichannel marketing opportunities and commercial challenges.

Best Practice Leadership: Lead a culture of best-practice sharing across the region, partnering with global digital teams to import and export proven growth strategies.

Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with Tech, Operations, and Finance teams to ensure the digital ecosystem supports the broader business modernization goals.

Tertiary level education in Business, Marketing, Technology, or a related field.

Minimum 10 years of experience in e-Commerce, digital platforms, or commercial leadership, preferably within QSR, FMCG, or retail.

A seasoned commercial leader with a proven track record of driving P&L growth and business performance through digital channels.

Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate complex data into sharp, executive-level business recommendations.

High level of maturity and credibility to influence franchise partners and senior regional stakeholders.

A track record of both strategic planning and galvanizing cross-functional teams (Tech, Ops, Marketing) to deliver executional excellence.

A growth and experimental mindset—not afraid to test hypotheses and stretch boundaries to achieve results.

Excellent interpersonal and communication skills with the ability to work effectively across diverse cultural backgrounds.

A high-energy "people grower" committed to building capability and leading teams toward a shared vision.

Assertive, with the confidence to be a voice of authority for the brand’s commercial and digital interests.

jobs.title: Product Manager Lead, Entertainment and Travel Ads
jobs.description:
Responsibilities
About the Team
TikTok is shaping the future of advertising and e-commerce on one of the world's leading platforms. The TikTok Content and Service Ads team, within the TikTok Monetization Product organization, is seeking a Product Leader to define and drive the vision, strategy, and execution of innovative advertising products serving web advertisers and travel and entertainment clients.
Our mission is to establish TikTok as the premier advertising platform for these verticals by delivering scalable, data-driven product solutions that accelerate advertiser growth, maximize performance, and create long-term business value.

Responsibilities
- Define and lead the long-term product vision, strategy, and roadmap for Web Ads and Open-Loop E-Commerce advertising solutions across travel, entertainment, and web advertiser segments.
- Own business outcomes, including revenue growth, advertiser satisfaction, retention, and overall platform adoption, while ensuring sustainable and scalable product development.
- Build, mentor, and inspire high-performing product management teams, fostering a culture of innovation, customer obsession, operational excellence, and accountability.
- Partner with executive leadership and cross-functional stakeholders to align product investments with company objectives and market opportunities.
- Drive the development of highly scalable machine learning–powered advertising products that improve advertiser performance and maximize return on ad spend (ROAS).
- Lead cross-functional teams across engineering, data science, design, operations, sales, and marketing to deliver end-to-end product solutions from concept through launch and continuous optimization.
- Establish frameworks for identifying market opportunities, customer pain points, and emerging industry trends, translating insights into impactful product strategies.
- Champion advertiser-centric innovation by developing advanced targeting, optimization, attribution, measurement, and product-matching capabilities.
- Ensure products adhere to evolving privacy regulations and industry standards while promoting responsible and effective use of advertising signals.
- Foster strong partnerships across Targeting, Attribution, Optimization, Insights, and other platform teams to unlock new capabilities and improve advertising performance globally.
- Serve as a thought leader and advocate for the travel, entertainment, and web advertising ecosystem, influencing product direction across the broader monetization organization.

Qualifications
Minimum Qualification(s)
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related technical field.
- 5+ years of product management experience, including significant experience leading advertising technology products and teams.
- Demonstrated success defining product vision, strategy, and execution for large-scale advertising, monetization, or machine learning–driven products.
- Deep expertise in the digital advertising ecosystem, preferably including ad delivery, targeting, attribution, optimization, and measurement systems.
- Strong understanding of the travel, entertainment, and/or web advertising industries and their evolving business needs.
- Experience leading global cross-functional initiatives involving engineering, data science, sales, operations, and business teams.
- Fluency in both English and Mandarin required: the role involves frequent communication with global teams, clients and stakeholders in English - speaking regions as well as Chinese- speaking markets. Proficiency in Mandarin is essential to effectively engage with Chinese- speaking partners, understand cultural nuances, and navigate business transactions in key markets, ensuring smooth cross- cultural collaboration and market expansion.

Preferred Qualification(s)
- MBA, Master's degree, or other advanced degree in a relevant field.
- Experience leading product organizations in a fast-growing technology company or fast-paced environment.
- Strong understanding of web advertiser needs, performance marketing, and e-commerce advertising solutions.
- Experience scaling international advertising products across multiple markets and regions.
- Demonstrated ability to drive organizational alignment and influence company-wide product strategy.

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​

Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.​
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an Always Day 1 mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​
​
Diversity & Inclusion​
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​
